title: Exchangeable seed replication under a locked tangent geometry
body: For each seed, Q2.6 computes the same mean six-slice replay gradient r and projects each scaled AdamW displacement d(alpha) as d'(alpha) = d(alpha) - max(0, dot(r,d(alpha))/dot(r,r)) r. The seed changes initialization randomness and sample order only. Per-seed go still requires the frozen public-and-promotion conjunction; family go is the conjunction of seed-level go for seeds 1, 2, and 3.
claims: The global tangent formula, direct 1.5% commit authority, and 2% public replay ceiling are seed-invariant.
The prospectively selected seed-2 model is the only promotion candidate; replication checkpoints cannot replace it post hoc.
Family no-go is valid if either replication seed completes without the public-and-promotion conjunction.
